title: Runtime Layer Schema
description: Execution control, revalidation, state management, and safe failure for Knowledge Blocks at runtime

retries.description: Number of retry attempts allowed on failure

parallel_execution.description: Whether execution may run in parallel with other instances

execution_window.description: Time window during which execution is permitted (ISO 8601 interval or cron expression)

human_review_required.description: Whether human review is required before execution proceeds

execution_control.description: Deterministic controls enforced at runtime before execution is allowed. These controls gate execution based on revalidation results, risk assessment, and policy consistency.

requires_revalidation.description: Whether Knowledge Block decision must be revalidated against current conditions before execution is permitted

required_authority.description: Required authority entities that must approve or validate execution (e.g., ['admin', 'compliance-officer']). Used with authority_mode.

authority_mode.description: Authority validation level: strict (revalidate authority at runtime), conditional (revalidate under specified conditions), advisory (no runtime re-check)

delta_threshold.description: Maximum tolerated drift between decision-time and execution-time state (0.0–1.0). Drift exceeding this may trigger revalidation or block execution.

delta_mode.description: Defines how aggressively state drift is interpreted relative to delta_threshold. 'strict' treats drift more seriously; 'tolerant' is more lenient.

evidence_freshness_window.description: Maximum allowed age of supporting evidence before revalidation is required (ISO 8601 duration, e.g., 'P30D', 'PT6H')

block_on_policy_mismatch.description: Whether execution must block if current organizational policy differs from decision-time policy

fail_behavior.description: Default response when execution control checks fail: block (prevent execution), degrade (reduced capability), warn (log and proceed), fallback (safe alternative), escalate (request human review)

allowed_actions.description: Actions explicitly permitted during execution (allowlist). If omitted, no explicit allowlist is enforced.

prohibited_actions.description: Actions explicitly forbidden during execution (blocklist). Used to prevent high-risk operations.

audit_log_required.description: Whether all execution decisions and outcomes must be logged immutably

revalidation.description: Revalidation rules applied at runtime against live conditions. If revalidation is enabled (requires_revalidation: true), these rules specify what to check and how to respond to mismatches.

compare_context.description: Revalidate current execution context against decision-time context

compare_policy.description: Revalidate current organizational policy against decision-time policy

compare_inputs.description: Revalidate current inputs for validity, type, and range against decision-time inputs

compare_risk_posture.description: Revalidate current runtime risk posture against acceptable thresholds

compare_evidence.description: Revalidate supporting evidence for freshness and continued correctness

on_mismatch.description: Response when revalidation detects a mismatch. Overrides execution_control.fail_behavior for revalidation-specific mismatches. block (prevent), degrade (reduced capability), warn (log and proceed), fallback (safe alternative), escalate (human review).

risk_posture.description: Runtime risk assessment applied at execution time. Risk posture captures current conditions that affect execution safety and tolerance.

impact.description: Potential severity if execution fails or causes harm

likelihood.description: Estimated probability of failure or negative outcome (0.0–1.0)

uncertainty.description: Confidence penalty for unknowns; higher values indicate greater uncertainty in the risk estimate

system_state.description: Current system environment: production carries highest risk, sandbox lowest

exposure_window.description: Duration the action's effects may persist: transient (seconds to minutes), session (user session), persistent (indefinite)

max_allowed_score.description: Risk score ceiling (0.0–1.0); execution blocks or escalates if runtime assessment exceeds this threshold

state.description: State machine and lifecycle management for Knowledge Block execution

initial_state.description: Starting state for this Knowledge Block's execution

state_machine.description: State machine definition with transitions and allowed actions per state

context.description: Runtime context: variables, inputs, and outputs

fallback_actions.description: Safe alternatives to execute if primary execution fails
